mechanism_explained: Roadmap explains the Java plus AI-verification course arc.
Setup becomes the first evidence-based verification habit.
Students learn to read running code as a business model before writing new code.
The chapter builds the reference-versus-object mental model needed for later debugging.
User interaction becomes an auditable path between object states.
The chapter teaches debugging as causal diagnosis rather than output patching.
The chapter separates durable business resources from user transactions.
Authentication becomes an object-model and risk-reasoning problem, not a pasted code block.
The chapter turns inheritance into a design strategy for extensible transaction behavior.
The chapter makes data lifecycle and failure states visible before persistence code is accepted.
The chapter connects collection type to access-pattern reasoning and performance consequences.
The chapter separates model truth from visual representation.
The chapter makes user actions traceable through event, handler, model update, and view refresh.
The chapter keeps visual design and controller logic connected but separate.
The chapter turns correctness from guesswork into stated and executable behavioral claims.
The chapter integrates Java implementation, object-oriented design, testing, GUI delivery, and accountable AI collaboration.
Appendix consolidates the course's fundamental themes.
